openmeter/ent.purpose: Organisational root for the Ent ORM persistence layer and the single source of truth for the database schema. openmeter/ent/schema owns every PostgreSQL table/view definition, openmeter/ent/tx bridges Ent transactions to the framework transaction.Creator, and the root entc.go drives codegen into the generated (DO-NOT-EDIT) openmeter/ent/db.

patterns.name: Schema-first generation
patterns.description: All database shapes live in openmeter/ent/schema/*.go; run `make generate` after any schema change and never edit openmeter/ent/db/. Atlas then diffs schema vs migration history.
patterns.example: go run -mod=readonly entc.go  // triggered by go:generate in generate.go

patterns.name: Extension-driven codegen in entc.go
patterns.description: entc.go is the sole codegen driver: it registers five custom extensions (entcursor, entexpose, entmixinaccessor, entpaginate, entsetorclear) and four Ent features (VersionedMigration, Lock, Upsert, ExecQuery). Any new feature/extension must be added here, not inline.

patterns.name: View SQL side-output
patterns.description: After Ent codegen, entc.go calls viewgen.GenerateFile to (re)produce tools/migrate/views.sql; view DDL is NOT in Atlas migrations and must never be hand-edited.

patterns.name: Transaction bridging via openmeter/ent/tx
patterns.description: Adapters obtain transactions through transaction.Creator (enttx.NewCreator) rather than db.Tx(ctx), so entutils.TransactingRepo can rebind the ctx-carried transaction.

key_file_guides.role: Single `go generate` entry point: configures all Ent features/extensions, targets ./db, and emits views.sql.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New Ent feature flags or extensions go here only; effective after `make generate`.

key_file_guides.role: One-line `//go:generate go run -mod=readonly entc.go` trigger consumed by make generate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Sole tx-package file; implements transaction.Creator via db.HijackTx + NewTxDriver so adapters bind to ctx-carried transactions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No business logic; never construct transactions elsewhere (e.g. db.Tx(ctx) in an adapter).

anti_patterns: Editing any file under openmeter/ent/db/ — fully generated and overwritten by make generate.
Calling db.Tx(ctx) directly in an adapter instead of going through openmeter/ent/tx's transaction.Creator — bypasses entutils.TransactingRepo rebinding.
Adding Ent feature flags or extensions outside entc.go — silently absent from generated code.
Hand-editing tools/migrate/views.sql — regenerated by entc.go on every make generate.
Skipping `make generate` after a schema change — openmeter/ent/db/ goes stale.

decisions.decision: entc.go is the single codegen driver rather than per-schema generators.
decisions.rationale: Centralises feature flags and the five custom extensions so they apply uniformly to every entity.

decisions.decision: Transaction bridging lives in a separate openmeter/ent/tx package.
decisions.rationale: Keeps transaction.Creator out of generated db code, preventing import cycles between generated code and pkg/framework.

openmeter/event.purpose: Organisational namespace for cross-subsystem event primitives shared by every Watermill producer. openmeter/event/metadata owns canonical event naming (io.openmeter.<subsystem>.<version>.<name>), resource-path construction (//openmeter.io/...), and EntityXxx constants; openmeter/event/models holds minimal validated payload value types (FeatureKeyAndID, NamespaceID). Both are leaf packages with no domain imports.

patterns.name: EventType triple for naming
patterns.description: Build all event names via metadata.EventType{Subsystem, Version, Name} + metadata.GetEventName; never assemble io.openmeter.* strings by hand. This name is what eventbus prefix-routing keys on.

patterns.name: Resource path construction
patterns.description: Build every //openmeter.io/... URI with metadata.ComposeResourcePath(namespace, entityConst, id); never fmt.Sprintf.

patterns.name: Entity constant registry
patterns.description: New entity types are registered as EntityXxx constants in metadata before use in event source/subject fields — never inline string literals in event structs.

patterns.name: Inline Validate() on every model type
patterns.description: Every exported type in openmeter/event/models implements Validate(), called at event deserialization boundaries to catch missing required fields.
patterns.example: func (m NamespaceID) Validate() error { if m.ID == "" { return errors.New(...) } }

key_file_guides.role: EventType, EventSubsystem, EventName, EventMetadata, GetEventName — the canonical naming contract for all events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New subsystems must follow io.openmeter.<subsystem>.<version>.<name>; version must be explicit (e.g. 'v1').

key_file_guides.role: ComposeResourcePath plus all EntityXxx constants for //openmeter.io/... URIs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Register a new EntityXxx constant before using a new entity in event source/subject.

key_file_guides.role: Cross-subsystem minimal payload types (FeatureKeyAndID, NamespaceID), each with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add domain-specific rich types (Invoice, Subscription) — they belong in their own packages and would create cycles.

anti_patterns: Building io.openmeter.* names by concatenation instead of EventType + GetEventName.
Constructing //openmeter.io/... paths with fmt.Sprintf instead of ComposeResourcePath.
Adding a new entity as an inline string literal instead of an EntityXxx constant in metadata.
Importing openmeter/* domain packages from openmeter/event/models — creates circular dependencies.
Skipping Validate() on payload values received from this package.

decisions.decision: Naming/routing (metadata) and payload value types (models) are kept in separate sub-packages.
decisions.rationale: Prevents the naming package from accumulating domain-specific types and keeps both as cycle-free leaves.

openmeter/info.purpose: Organisational namespace for the info domain. It currently contains only openmeter/info/httpdriver, which serves stateless endpoints (e.g. currency list) through the httptransport pattern with no domain service, adapter, or Wire provider — data comes directly from external libraries (gobl/currency) or static definitions.

patterns.name: Dependency-free stateless handlers
patterns.description: When an endpoint's data is static/library-sourced, the handler struct holds no injected service so it stays trivially testable.
patterns.example: type handler struct{} // operation func calls gobl/currency.All() directly

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler triple
patterns.description: Every endpoint follows the decoder/operation/encoder triple: decoder extracts params, operation is pure logic, encoder serialises.

patterns.name: No-op decoder for parameterless endpoints
patterns.description: Endpoints without params use a decoder returning an empty struct and nil error rather than reading the request.

patterns.name: Handler interface + private struct
patterns.description: driver.go declares the public Handler interface and New constructor; per-resource files (currencies.go) implement a private handler struct.

key_file_guides.role: Public Handler interface and New constructor — the only symbols exported to router.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new endpoints to the Handler interface here before implementing them in a per-resource file.

key_file_guides.role: ListCurrencies — canonical no-service stateless handler sourcing data from gobl/currency.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not inject a service/adapter; data needing DB access belongs in a new domain package, not here.

anti_patterns: Injecting a domain service or adapter when data comes from a static library.
Returning a raw error from the operation func instead of a typed domain error — breaks the GenericErrorEncoder chain.
Hand-editing generated API types in api/ instead of regenerating via make gen-api.
Putting request validation in the decoder func instead of returning a validation error from the operation func.

decisions.decision: No domain service or Wire provider for info/httpdriver.
decisions.rationale: All endpoints are static library-backed; a Wire provider for static data would add needless DI boilerplate.

openmeter/llmcost.purpose: LLM cost-price domain: persists global (synced, namespace IS NULL, source='system') prices and per-namespace manual overrides (source='manual') in the llmcostprice table, resolves effective prices with namespace-override precedence, and syncs prices from external sources (models.dev). All monetary values use alpacadecimal.Decimal.

patterns.name: Validate() + Nillable wrapping on every input
patterns.description: Each input type asserts `var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil)` and its Validate() returns models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)); call input.Validate() at the service boundary.

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinels for field errors
patterns.description: Each field-level error is a named sentinel in errors.go combining an ErrCodeXxx, field path, severity, and HTTP-status attribute.
patterns.example: var ErrProviderEmpty = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeProviderEmpty, "provider must not be empty", models.WithFieldString("provider"), models.WithCriticalSeverity(), commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))

patterns.name: PriceSource discrimination (system vs manual)
patterns.description: Global rows are namespace IS NULL + source='system'; overrides are namespace IS NOT NULL + source='manual'. Never mix these in one query path; UpsertGlobalPrice is reserved for system rows.

patterns.name: alpacadecimal.Decimal for all price fields
patterns.description: Cost-per-token fields use alpacadecimal.Decimal (never float64/string); optional dimensions (CacheRead/CacheWrite/Reasoning) are *alpacadecimal.Decimal.

patterns.name: NormalizeModelID before lookup/insert
patterns.description: Call llmcost.NormalizeModelID(provider, modelID) before storing or resolving to canonicalise casing, version/region suffixes, and provider aliases uniformly.

patterns.name: Override overlay at service layer; sync requires multi-source agreement
patterns.description: Adapter keeps global/override as separate query paths; the service batch-fetches overrides once and overlays them in memory. The sync pipeline (Fetch→Normalize→Deduplicate→Reconcile) writes globals only after minAgreement sources agree within tolerance.
patterns.example: // service overlays overrides; sync calls Adapter.UpsertGlobalPrice after reconciler.Reconcile

key_file_guides.role: Core domain types: Price, ModelPricing, PriceSource, SourcePrice, SourcePricesMap with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a token-cost dimension requires updating ModelPricing, Validate(), and all mapping/adapter code.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface plus input types with Validate() and compile-time Validator assertions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new Service method needs a matching XInput with Validate() and an assertion.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter interface (extends entutils.TxCreator) — source of truth for persistence operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New queries/writes must be declared here before implementing in adapter/.

key_file_guides.role: All ValidationIssue sentinels and error constructors for the domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New field errors follow the ErrCodeXxx + NewValidationIssue + explicit HTTP status pattern.

key_file_guides.role: NormalizeModelID/NormalizeProvider: strip version/region suffixes, normalise aliases. Must precede any store/resolve.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New aliases or suffix patterns require updating this file and its test table.

key_file_guides.role: Ent adapter; all writes wrapped in TransactingRepo; ResolvePrice uses ORDER BY namespace DESC to prefer overrides; soft-delete via SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call a.db directly in a write helper outside TransactingRepo; never hard-delete.

key_file_guides.role: Four-phase pipeline writing globals only after multi-source agreement.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Fetcher errors must be non-fatal; reconciler.Reconcile must run after deduplicateSourcePrices.

anti_patterns: Using float64/string for price fields instead of alpacadecimal.Decimal.
Skipping NormalizeModelID before storing/resolving prices.
Calling a.db directly in adapter write helpers without TransactingRepo.
Adding source='manual' rows via UpsertGlobalPrice (reserved for reconciled system prices).
Calling reconciler.Reconcile before deduplicateSourcePrices — allows false multi-source agreement from aliasing.

decisions.decision: Namespace-override overlay applied at the service layer, not the adapter.
decisions.rationale: Keeps adapter queries simple (separate global/override paths); the service batches and merges in memory.

decisions.decision: Require multi-source agreement before writing global prices.
decisions.rationale: A single down or rogue source must not pollute globals; minAgreement within priceTolerance guards this.

decisions.decision: Normalisation is a pure package-level function (NormalizeModelID).
decisions.rationale: Applied uniformly by both sync and adapter/service, eliminating per-caller key-construction drift.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service method with input validation

openmeter/meter.purpose: Meter domain: defines usage aggregation rules (event type, COUNT/SUM/MAX/UNIQUE_COUNT, group-by JSON paths), manages meter lifecycle with soft-delete, validates events against meters at ingest via ParseEvent, and publishes Watermill events after mutations. Service is read-only; ManageService embeds it and adds mutation, pre-update hooks, and event publishing consumed by billing/entitlement.

patterns.name: Service/ManageService split
patterns.description: Read-only callers depend on meter.Service; mutating callers depend on meter.ManageService (embeds Service + CreateMeter/UpdateMeter/DeleteMeter/RegisterHooks). Wire injects ManageService where mutation is needed.
patterns.example: type ManageService interface { Service; CreateMeter(...); UpdateMeter(...); DeleteMeter(...) }

patterns.name: Validate() on every input type
patterns.description: CreateMeterInput/UpdateMeterInput/DeleteMeterInput/GetMeterInput/ListMetersParams each have explicit Validate() called before any adapter/service logic.

patterns.name: ValueProperty/GroupBy invariants enforced on both model and input
patterns.description: COUNT forbids ValueProperty; other aggregations require it with a '$' prefix; GroupBy values start with '$' and keys match groupByKeyRegExp. Checked in both Meter.Validate() and CreateMeterInput.Validate() via shared helpers.
patterns.example: if aggregation == MeterAggregationCount && valueProperty != nil { return errors.New(...) }

patterns.name: Watermill event publishing after mutations
patterns.description: The service layer (not the adapter) publishes MeterCreate/Update/DeleteEvent after a successful adapter write, using the metadata.EventType triple and ComposeResourcePath.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt
patterns.description: Meters are never hard-deleted; ListMeters excludes soft-deleted unless IncludeDeleted=true; DeleteMeter sets DeletedAt and is idempotent.

patterns.name: ParseEvent for ingest validation
patterns.description: meter.ParseEvent(meter, data) validates and extracts value/group-by from CloudEvent JSON; ErrInvalidEvent=data error, ErrInvalidMeter=config error. COUNT meters get Value=1.0; UNIQUE_COUNT get ValueString.
patterns.example: parsedEvent, err := meter.ParseEvent(m, rawData) // *ParsedEvent{Value, ValueString, GroupBy}

key_file_guides.role: Service/ManageService interfaces plus all input types with Validate() — the meter API surface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithoutNamespace in ListMetersParams is the only safe cross-namespace listing flag — never pass empty namespace without it.

key_file_guides.role: Meter type, MeterAggregation/WindowSize enums, aggregation/group-by validation helpers, MeterQueryRow.
key_file_guides.watch_for: validateMeterAggregation/validateMeterGroupBy are shared by Meter.Validate() and CreateMeterInput.Validate() — keep in sync.

key_file_guides.role: ParseEvent/ParseEventString extracting value/group-by at ingest.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing GROUP BY fields return empty string (not error) intentionally — do not change.

key_file_guides.role: MeterCreate/Update/DeleteEvent implementing EventName()/EventMetadata() for Watermill via ComposeResourcePath.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeterDeleteEvent.Validate() requires non-nil DeletedAt — set by the adapter before constructing the event.

key_file_guides.role: MeterNotFoundError wrapping GenericNotFoundError + IsMeterNotFoundError helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use IsMeterNotFoundError for type-checked matching rather than errors.As on the inner error.

key_file_guides.role: Ent adapter enforcing soft-delete, namespace isolation, and TransactingRepo wrapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All queries need DeletedAt IS NULL unless IncludeDeleted; never call a.db outside TransactingRepo.

key_file_guides.role: In-memory test mock; optional PG sync via SetDBClient for FK constraints.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Test-only; SetDBClient must precede seeding to sync; RegisterHooks is a noop in the mock.

anti_patterns: Passing empty Namespace to ListMeters without WithoutNamespace=true.
Adding DB queries directly in service/manage.go — persistence goes through the adapter.
Publishing events before the adapter mutation succeeds.
Skipping validateJSONPaths (streaming.Connector) before CreateMeter/UpdateMeter — invalid paths fail silently until query time.
Hard-deleting meter rows instead of setting DeletedAt — breaks FK integrity and idempotent delete.

decisions.decision: The service layer publishes Watermill events after adapter mutations, not the adapter.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the adapter purely persistence; event publishing is a cross-cutting orchestration concern.

decisions.decision: ManageService holds a direct *adapter.Adapter alongside the embedded Service.
decisions.rationale: Lets ManageService call write/soft-delete helpers not on the read-only Service interface without bloating it.

decisions.decision: mockadapter PG sync is opt-in via SetDBClient rather than always requiring a DB.
decisions.rationale: Most unit tests need only in-memory resolution; tests with feature meter_id FKs opt in.

code_examples.scenario: Publishing a domain event after a meter mutation
